steps.title: Fastest path: translate the equation first
steps.content: Use the given trigonometric relationship directly. Since both angles are acute, the complementary-angle identity connects sine and cosine.

steps.title: sin X = cos Y means X + Y = 90°
steps.content: $\frac{\sin X}{\cos Y} = 1$ → $\sin X = \cos Y$. The complementary identity: if $\sin A = \cos B$ (both acute), then $A + B = 90^{\circ}$.
